establishment: NOT ESTABLISHED -- output of RDTG_recompute.py; every number and every verdict below is an OUTPUT, not established, until the RDT-G verifier scenario runs (build/reserve/RDTG_verify.json with all_pass=true, byte-reproducing this file and the amended object from the committed inputs) AND the human gate reviews this stage.

M1_external_managers_custodians.consequence_pre_committed: a null on any leg (and on the sweep) is WEAK evidence, not disposal.
M1_external_managers_custodians.grounding: IMF Revised Guidelines (2013) + US Treasury/TIC SHL2024r 'custodial bias' passages, verbatim in RDTG_legs_manifest.json (retained PDFs in rdtg_evidence/)
M1_external_managers_custodians.tic_quote: This "custodial bias" tends to overstate the amounts of holdings by residents of countries with major custodial activities such as Belgium, Luxembourg, Switzerland, and the United Kingdom.

M2_counterpart_attribution.consequence_pre_committed: a surge is CONSISTENCY, not attribution; the absence of a China line is not the absence of China.
M2_counterpart_attribution.grounding: compiler-side statements retained per leg (BoJ FAQ Q10; ECB quality report fn. 8; ONS CPIS-mirror; StatCan IMDB 1535; ABS Form 85L; BdF custodian statements) -- RDTG_legs_manifest.json

mass_and_bars.bars_artifact: build/reserve/RDTG_bars.json (committed BEFORE any discriminator was read; sequencing attestation carried there)
mass_and_bars.stated_limitation_direction: Pre-committed direction: the candidate set is not the universe of non-US destinations (corporates, equities, deposits, non-candidate sovereigns absorb too), so per-market bars OVERSTATE what any one market should show -- which makes a null WEAKER, never stronger.

no_date_no_probability_no_currency_guess: no breaking-point date, no probability, and no destination-currency guess anywhere in the RDT-G outputs (the k1 wall stands)

part_a_ib_verdict.SOURCE: build/reserve/RDTG_ib_series.csv | 132 SAFE SDDS templates (RDT-D corpus, disk-only) | re-derived here and cross-checked against RDTG_ib_staging.json | read 2026-07-02
part_a_ib_verdict.axis: I.B (SAFE SDDS Section I.B, 'other foreign currency assets'), monthly, USD bn

disclosure_finding.SOURCE: build/reserve/RDTG_ib_series.csv (re-derived here) cross-checked against build/reserve/RDTG_ib_staging.json; upstream corpus build/reserve/rdtd_evidence/
disclosure_finding.finding: DISCLOSURE finding riding the I.B verdict (not a SUPPRESSED verdict -- the total is published in all 132 months): gold and 'other' sub-lines are BLANK in 132/132 and 132/132 months; deposits BLANK in 131/132 (single explicit 0 in 2017-04); loans BLANK in 2025-11, 2025-12, 2026-01, 2026-02, 2026-05 (explicit 0.0 in 2026-03..04); securities published in all 132 months and carries essentially the whole line. Blanks are a disclosure state, never interpolated.

sub_lines_reported_wherever_published.ib_deposits / ib_gold / ib_other: no published verdict-window values (see blank counts)
sub_lines_reported_wherever_published.ib_fin_derivatives: explicit values (incl. 0.0) most months; verdict-window delta 0.0

ib_loans_usd_bn.note: re-derived from the committed CSV (flags cross-checked against the staging above); latest month BLANK so no verdict-window delta

part_a_ib_verdict.perimeter: I.B movement is a within-China's-own-books discriminator (re-parking into non-reserve FX assets would surface here); it attributes nothing beyond China's own reporting perimeter (pre-registered).
part_a_ib_verdict.verdict_sentence: MECHANICAL I.B VERDICT: FLAT -- ΔI.B_total over the verdict window (2022-01 -> 2026-05, latest published) = -18.696 $bn, strictly between -24.749 and +24.749 (threshold reproduced at run time as 0.05 x 494.977, the RDTD upper-mass field); endpoint-convention variant (2021-12 base): -18.740 $bn -- FLAT under BOTH conventions. The sign is NEGATIVE (I.B fell) but does not reach the -24.749 DECLINE bar. SUPPRESSED does not attach to the total (published in all 132 months); the persistent sub-line blanks ride as a DISCLOSURE finding.

e_bund_investor_structure_official_split.class: AGGREGATE (with an OFFICIAL third-country split -- the closest thing to an official split in the sweep)
e_bund_investor_structure_official_split.construction: SHARES (% of total volume of Federal securities, whole percent, point-in-time table 2020-12..2025-06). Conversion to EUR amounts per the tasking uses the leg's own publisher denominator: only the end-2021 outstanding (EUR 1589.900 bn incl own holdings, RDTG_denominators.json, same publisher) is grounded, so EUR equivalents are YARDSTICK-ONLY conversions at the CONSTANT end-2021 outstanding, stated; pp moves are the primary numbers.
e_bund_investor_structure_official_split.custody_caveat: Finanzagentur estimation-basis statement + Bundesbank 2018 custody-channel corroboration verbatim in RDTG_legs_manifest.json
e_bund_investor_structure_official_split.powered: NOT-COMPUTABLE -- excluded from powered counts, exclusion stated
e_bund_investor_structure_official_split.prominent_context_reading: The OFFICIAL third-country line -- 'Central banks and government sector - third countries' -- FELL from 17% (2021-12) to 14% (2025-06) of Federal securities (-3 pp, ~-47.697 EUR bn at the constant end-2021 outstanding, yardstick only), while 'Other investors - third countries' ROSE 7% -> 16% (+9 pp). The only official-sector split in the sweep moved AWAY from official third-country holders over the verdict window -- direction ABSENT-side; never counted (power not computable); the publisher's 2024 model revision rides the series.
e_bund_investor_structure_official_split.status: GROUNDED-PRIMARY -- POWER NOT-COMPUTABLE: baseline support is the single annual change 2020-12->2021-12, so sigma_baseline cannot be computed from one change and the power test cannot run; the leg is reported with its numbers but EXCLUDED from powered counts (within the pre-registered rule: unpowered/untestable legs report, never count)
e_bund_investor_structure_official_split.windows: baseline 2020-12-31->2021-12-31 ONLY (nearest published boundaries); verdict 2021-12-31->2025-06-30 (nearest published boundary), 3.5 years

f_jgb_overseas_holdings.SOURCE: build/reserve/rdtg_evidence/leg_f_boj_fof2_en.zip :: ff_dl_fof_quarterly_en.csv rows FOF_FFAS500A311/310, FOF_FFALC08G500 | BoJ Flow of Funds | parsed 2026-07-02
f_jgb_overseas_holdings.construction: POSITIONS (stocks), quarterly, unit 100 million yen (verified from the retained BoJ sjpre_units.xlsx header) converted to JPY bn. HEADLINE row: FOF_FFAS500A311 'Central government securities and FILP bonds / Overseas / Stock' -- the perimeter closest to the bar's MoF General-Bonds outstanding denominator (which excludes Treasury discount bills); the T-bill row and the combined sum are carried as stated variants; the from-whom-to-whom Overseas row equals the two stock rows at both window endpoints (guarded cross-check). FILP-bond inclusion in the FOF row vs the General-Bonds denominator is a stated perimeter friction.
f_jgb_overseas_holdings.custody_caveat: BoJ FOF guide (Overseas sector = BOP nonresidents) + BoJ FAQ Q10, verbatim in RDTG_legs_manifest.json
f_jgb_overseas_holdings.reading: Overseas holdings of JGBs+FILP bonds FELL 6255.800 JPY bn over the verdict window against a +5866.986/yr baseline mean -- excess -29723.743 JPY bn, deeply below the bar. The combined-incl-bills variant lands the same side. M1/M2 caveats ride (the FOF Overseas sector inherits the BOP custodian-attribution perimeter).

treasury_discount_bills_row (FOF_FFAS500.note: unpowered under the Scheme-1 bar (sigma exceeds it); reported only

g_gilt_overseas_holdings.construction: POSITIONS, GBP bn, ONS CDID HEWD (UKEA), quarterly, MARKET VALUE current prices -- the bars are built on NOMINAL outstandings: a stated basis friction (the tonnage-not-value lesson says flag it and compute on what the leg publishes; the 2022 gilt-price fall moves this series without any tonnage change). Annual rows equal Q4 rows (guarded).
g_gilt_overseas_holdings.custody_caveat: no custody-specific ONS caveat found for HEWD; the same compiler's CPIS-mirror attribution basis (leg c) stated in RDTG_legs_manifest.json; DMO revision note quoted there
g_gilt_overseas_holdings.reading: Overseas gilt holdings at market value were near-FLAT over the verdict window (-3.642 GBP bn on a 740.997 base) against a +48.764/yr baseline mean -- excess -198.696 GBP bn -- but sigma_baseline 29.873 exceeds Bar_low 18.299, so the leg is UNPOWERED and never counted; the market-value basis makes both the baseline sigma and the verdict change price-contaminated (stated).
g_gilt_overseas_holdings.status: GROUNDED-PRIMARY -- TESTED; UNPOWERED at both bars (sigma_baseline exceeds Bar_low under Scheme 1 and Scheme 2): reported, never counted

bdf_chart_general_government_nonresident.note: compiler chart data, 2022Q3->2025Q3 only (verdict-window-partial; perimeter differs from the State-debt share); CONTEXT ONLY, no baseline exists on disk so no test is run

context_only_no_test.publisher_metadata_last_two_obs_of_the_b: 56.0, 55.1 (publisher metadata field series_last_two_obs_values; order as published)

h_oat_nonresident_share.custody_caveat: BdF Stat Info holdings-from-custodian-statements sentence verbatim in RDTG_legs_manifest.json
h_oat_nonresident_share.status: NOT-AVAILABLE -- the series exists at the publisher but is unfetchable from this egress (AFT Cloudflare-blocked; BdF webstat exports empty portal-wide; BdF API requires credentials -- evidence retained); no baseline -> no test; reported, never counted

i_ags_nonresident_gg_debt_securities.construction: HEADLINE: POSITIONS, end-of-period (ABS measure 6, 'Position at end of period'), AUD bn, market-value IIP convention; the leg is GENERAL GOVERNMENT total (AGS plus state/territory issuers -- broader than AGS-only, dilution stated). FLOW-TEST VARIANT: transactions (measure 2, 'Changes in position reflecting - transactions'), both reported. Beginning/end-of-period coherence guarded.
i_ags_nonresident_gg_debt_securities.custody_caveat: ABS Form-85L nominee-survey sentence verbatim in RDTG_legs_manifest.json
i_ags_nonresident_gg_debt_securities.reading: Nonresident holdings of Australian general-government debt securities rose +33.231 AUD bn over the verdict window, below the baseline-trend expectation (excess -55.191 positions / -15.477 transactions), but sigma_baseline (47.102 positions / 38.669 transactions) exceeds the Scheme-2 bar_low 10.313 -- UNPOWERED both ways, never counted.
i_ags_nonresident_gg_debt_securities.status: GROUNDED-ALTERNATE-OFFICIAL (ABS 5302.0 IIP; AOFM primary blocked) -- TESTED; UNPOWERED at the Scheme-2 bar in BOTH variants: reported, never counted. Scheme-1 bar NOT-COMPUTABLE for AU (outstanding NOT-GROUNDED) -> the Scheme-2 AUD bar is the power yardstick, stated.

test.bar_low_scheme1_local_bn: NOT-COMPUTABLE (AGS outstanding NOT-GROUNDED; Scheme-2 AUD bar used, stated)

i_ags_nonresident_gg_debt_securities.windows: baseline Q4/2014->Q4/2021 annual changes; verdict 2021Q4->2025Q4 (transactions variant: annual sums / cumulative)

a_japan_bop_vs_china_pi_liabilities.SOURCE: build/reserve/rdtg_evidence/leg_a_boj_regbp_q_en.zip :: regbp_q_en.csv rows BPBP6QFLCN2/BPBP6QFLCN22 | BoJ/MoF regional BOP | parsed 2026-07-02
a_japan_bop_vs_china_pi_liabilities.construction: FLOWS, stated as such (mechanical extension of the pre-registered level-change construction: Japan publishes bilateral vis-a-vis-China portfolio-investment TRANSACTIONS, not positions, in the regional BOP; cumulative net incurrence of liabilities over a window is the level-change equivalent under zero valuation effects -- positions preferred per the pre-registration but not published on this line). Series BPBP6QFLCN2, quarterly, 100 million Yen converted to JPY bn.
a_japan_bop_vs_china_pi_liabilities.custody_caveat: BoJ FAQ Q10 verbatim in RDTG_legs_manifest.json (transactor-economy attribution; foreign custodian redemptions attribute to the custodian)
a_japan_bop_vs_china_pi_liabilities.reading: China's net transactions in Japanese portfolio securities were NEGATIVE over the verdict window (net sales), against a positive baseline mean -- the excess is deeply negative. M2 caveat rides: transactions via non-China intermediaries attribute to the intermediary's economy, so this line's absence is not the absence of China.
a_japan_bop_vs_china_pi_liabilities.windows: baseline 2015Q1->2021Q4 annual flow sums; verdict 2022Q1->2025Q4 cumulative

b_euroarea_bop_china_pi_liabilities.construction: POSITIONS, EUR bn, market value, quarterly (ECB BP6, EA19 vis-a-vis China, PI liabilities; every observation flagged E=estimated)
b_euroarea_bop_china_pi_liabilities.custody_caveat: ECB quality report June 2024 footnote 8 verbatim in RDTG_legs_manifest.json (residual estimation of PI liabilities)
b_euroarea_bop_china_pi_liabilities.status: NOT-TESTABLE -- the position series ends 2022Q3 (3 verdict quarters of 16); the pre-registered test cannot run; reported, never counted
b_euroarea_bop_china_pi_liabilities.structural_absence_finding: The euro-area flows-by-counterpart LIABILITIES cell does not exist: Eurostat bop_eu6_q returns 130 observations for the China-partner portfolio ASSETS flow and 0 for LIABILITIES (probes retained), and the ECB position series stops at 2022Q3. A China-attributed leg whose absence is itself informative about the compiler perimeter: euro-area compilers estimate portfolio liabilities RESIDUALLY (quality-report footnote, retained) because end-holder residency is not observable -- the cell's absence is a disclosure/methodology fact, not evidence of absence of flows.
b_euroarea_bop_china_pi_liabilities.windows: baseline 2014Q4->2021Q4 (computable, context); verdict window essentially unsupported (published boundary 2022Q3)

c_uk_pinkbook_china_pi_liabilities.SOURCE: build/reserve/rdtg_evidence/leg_c_ons_pinkbook{2016..2025}_chapter10.xls[x] :: sheet 10.1 China row | ONS Pink Book | parsed 2026-07-02
c_uk_pinkbook_china_pi_liabilities.construction: POSITIONS, GBP bn (Pink Book table 10.1, China row, 'Portfolio investment liabilities' column; one reference year per edition, stitched PB2016->PB2025; end-2014 cell is '-' (nil/<GBP 0.05bn))
c_uk_pinkbook_china_pi_liabilities.custody_caveat: ONS Pink Book 2014 CPIS-mirror statement verbatim in RDTG_legs_manifest.json
c_uk_pinkbook_china_pi_liabilities.reading: The China line ROSE (18.600 -> 27.200 GBP bn, +8.600 over 2 years; excess +5.367 above baseline trend) -- a real, above-trend rise, but below the leg-CONSISTENT bar (0.5 x Bar_low = 9.149): the Scheme-1 bar_low alone (18.299) is 98.382% of the ENTIRE end-2021 China line (18.600), and bar_high (64.261) is a multiple of it. Rendered leg-ABSENT by the pre-registered rule; the rise is reported, not suppressed. M2 caveat rides: the Pink Book China row is a CPIS-mirror construction and center-routed holdings attribute to the center.
c_uk_pinkbook_china_pi_liabilities.status: GROUNDED-PRIMARY -- TESTED (verdict window TRUNCATED to end-2023, the nearest published boundary; mixed-vintage friction carried: each year's value comes from a different edition)
c_uk_pinkbook_china_pi_liabilities.windows: baseline end-2015->end-2021 (6 annual changes); verdict end-2021->end-2023

d_canada_securities_transactions_china.custody_caveat: StatCan IMDB 1535 intermediary-collection statement verbatim in RDTG_legs_manifest.json
d_canada_securities_transactions_china.status: NOT-AVAILABLE -- absence by construction, not a fetch failure: StatCan's international-securities geographic split has no China line (China sits inside 'All other countries'); reported, never counted

part_b_leg_tests.rule: Per grounded leg, local currency: excess = verdict-window change - baseline mean annual change x verdict years; POWERED iff Bar_low >= sigma_baseline; leg-CONSISTENT iff excess >= 0.5 x Bar_low (powered); leg-ABSENT iff excess < 0.5 x Bar_low (powered); unpowered/untestable legs report, never count. Scheme 1 bars = headline; Scheme 2 alongside. China-attributed legs reported separately from aggregate legs.

part_c_sweep_verdict.preregistered_expectation_check: Pre-registered primary was 'UNPOWERED or INDETERMINATE on the sweep and FLAT-or-SUPPRESSED on I.B'. I.B landed FLAT (the expected side). The Scheme-1 sweep landed DESTINATION-ABSENT -- the pre-registered primary is REFUTED toward DESTINATION-ABSENT on the pre-stated branch ('REFUTED toward DESTINATION-ABSENT if powered legs sit flat across the sweep'); the Scheme-2 variant lands the expected UNPOWERED. All landings promotable; recorded, not smoothed.
part_c_sweep_verdict.rule: DESTINATION-CONSISTENT iff >= half of powered grounded legs are leg-CONSISTENT or any China-attributed leg is; DESTINATION-ABSENT iff >= three-quarters are leg-ABSENT and no China-attributed leg is leg-CONSISTENT (the verdict sentence itself carries the masking caveat); UNPOWERED iff fewer than 3 powered grounded legs; INDETERMINATE otherwise (pre-registered)

scheme2_variant.statement: Scheme-2 (COFER-share) bars variant: UNPOWERED -- only 1 powered grounded leg(s) (c) because the JPY bar shrinks to the COFER JPY share; the headline sweep is Scheme 1 (market-size-proportional) as tasked, the variant stated alongside.

part_c_sweep_verdict.verdict_sentence: MECHANICAL SWEEP VERDICT: DESTINATION-ABSENT -- 3 of 3 powered grounded legs are leg-ABSENT (>= three-quarters) and no China-attributed leg is leg-CONSISTENT -- AND, per the pre-registration, this verdict sentence carries its own masking caveat: a null on any leg (and on the sweep) is WEAK evidence, not disposal. Reserve assets bought through external managers, custodians or financial centers would NOT appear on these legs (M1: the destination compilers' own 'custodial bias'; M2: a surge is CONSISTENCY, not attribution; the absence of a China line is not the absence of China.) -- so DESTINATION-ABSENT is weak evidence against arrival in these six sovereign markets under these compilers' attribution, not disposal of the true-departure branch.

part_d_fork_interval_annotation.annotation: weight toward RE-PARKING, MASKING-CAPPED (the pre-committed consequence of DESTINATION-ABSENT: a null is weak evidence, not disposal)
part_d_fork_interval_annotation.annotation_with_ib: Both untried discriminators landed on the same side and neither resolves the fork: the destination sweep is DESTINATION-ABSENT (masking-capped) and China's own I.B line is FLAT (slightly negative) -- no visible arrival in the candidate sovereign markets under their compilers' attribution, and no re-parking surge into China's own disclosed non-reserve FX assets. The pre-committed consequence rule moves weight toward re-parking/masking-capped readings of the fork; the interval's endpoints stand at 48.484..494.977 $bn.
part_d_fork_interval_annotation.endpoints: UNCHANGED -- no leg carried construction-grade attribution (the pre-registration: no receiving leg above can; and no China-attributed leg is leg-CONSISTENT). Expected amendment: annotation only -- met.

masking_consequences_pre_committed.M1: a null on any leg (and on the sweep) is WEAK evidence, not disposal.
masking_consequences_pre_committed.M2: a surge is CONSISTENCY, not attribution; the absence of a China line is not the absence of China.

windows.baseline: calendar 2015->2021 (leg-specific nearest published boundaries stated per leg)
windows.note: RDT-G freeze-era windows, deliberately distinct from the RDT-B..F monthly verdict axis; both facts stated (pre-registered).
windows.verdict_ib: 2022-01 -> latest published (2026-05), per the pre-registered I.B wording; both endpoint conventions reported
